----- PRIOR SESSION 139 -----
Session 139 -- US-267 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 22 discriminator C: fsync stage-row writes + audit error propagation -- the LAST of Spool's discriminator trio US-265 + US-266 + US-267 ahead of Drain Test 7). TWO artifacts: src/pi/power/power_db.py UPDATE (logShutdownStage hardened with the full hypothesis-C durability chain -- `conn.execute("PRAGMA synchronous = FULL")` flips WAL-mode default of NORMAL so SQLite fsyncs WAL on every commit; explicit `conn.commit()` after INSERT; defense-in-depth `os.fsync(fd)` on a fresh `os.open(database.dbPath, O_RDONLY)` after with-block exits, wrapped in its own try/except OSError so fsync failure logs ERROR but does NOT roll back the row; outer try/except now catches Exception, logs ERROR with `exc_info=True`, AND RE-RAISES -- the contract inversion that closes the silent-swallow class; +os import; mod-history entry; +56 lines including substantial docstring expansion documenting the 4-step chain and the safety-ladder coupling with orchestrator's _writePowerLogStage), tests/pi/power/test_logshutdown_stage_fsync_and_error_propagation.py NEW (7 tests across 3 classes / ~430 lines): TestLogShutdownStageFsync (3 tests -- callsOsFsyncAtLeastOnce via patch wraps + setsPragmaSynchronousFull via _SqlTracker delegating-proxy class because sqlite3.Connection.execute is read-only and cannot be setattr'd + walMode_rowVisibleFromSecondConnection regression-coverage); TestLogShutdownStageErrorPropagation (2 tests -- sqlite3.OperationalError + DatabaseConnectionError both re-raise with ERROR log captured at logger='src.pi.power.power_db'); TestStairStepDrainRowsPresentAfterWriterReturns (2 tests -- full orchestrator stair-step 4.20->3.40V drain with rows-visible-after-each-tick assertion + idempotency invariant locked per Spool's spec). IMPORTANT FIXTURE GOTCHA (logged in progress.txt learnings): the orchestrator's tick() imports PowerSource from `src.pi.hardware.ups_monitor` (line 378) NOT from `src.pi.power.types`; tests passing `currentSource=PowerSource.BATTERY` from the wrong path silently hit the `currentSource != _PS.BATTERY` early-return and bail without firing transitions. First test run failed for this reason; fix was a one-line import path correction. Verification: tests/pi/power/test_logshutdown_stage_fsync_and_error_propagation.py 7/7 PASS in 45.31s post-fix; pi/power + pi/diagnostics 159 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failures in 91.18s; full fast suite 3845 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 987.77s (16:27) -- baseline post-US-266 was 3838 pass / 26 fail, delta +7 = exactly the new test count + ZERO regressions; the 26 failures unchanged TD-042 + TD-044 set (closed by US-268 + US-269 later this sprint). Runtime-validation gate per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md: `git stash push -- src/pi/power/power_db.py` reverted the fix and re-ran the 7 tests; 4 FAILED as expected (callsOsFsyncAtLeastOnce: AttributeError on missing os module attr; setsPragmaSynchronousFull: empty executed-SQL list; both error-propagation tests: "DID NOT RAISE") and 3 still PASSED (visibility-from-second-connection + stair-step end-to-end + idempotency are pre-fix invariants and regression-coverage post-fix). Restored the fix; 7/7 pass again. Ruff one auto-fix (I001 import-sort on test file). sprint_lint US-267 OK; only carryover warning is US-263 sizing. SPOOL DISCRIMINATOR TRIO NOW FULLY WIRED ahead of Drain Test 7: post-drain Spool's truth-table verdict reads as -- pd_tick_count stays 0 means US-265 fix (thread-liveness), pd_tick_count increments + pd_stage stays NORMAL means US-266 fix (silent-bail), pd_stage advances + power_log has STAGE_* rows means US-267 fix (write-path) + the orchestrator-side ERROR log will name any previously-swallowed exception class. Pattern carryforward: defense-in-depth durability chain (PRAGMA synchronous=FULL + explicit commit + os.fsync) wraps cleanly with two narrow try/except boundaries -- outer Exception around INSERT path RE-RAISES (loud, satisfies never-swallow contract); inner OSError around os.fsync does NOT re-raise (row is already SQLite-durable, fsync failure is best-effort). Reusable for any future forensics writer where the row is the load-bearing artifact and fsync is defense-in-depth. Phantom-path drift broke its 9-session pattern this round: scope.filesToTouch listed real paths (no silent-substitute needed). Sprint 22 status NOW: 6/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265 + US-266 + US-267 -- the discriminator trio is COMPLETE plus the forensics logger + boot-reason + dashboard-fix); remaining 2 unclaimed -- US-268 (M, P0; TD-042 mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures + brings fast suite to fully-green) + US-269 (S, P0; TD-044 one-liner SemVer-shape regex on test_migration_0005). Recommended next-iteration pick: US-268 (M; the larger TD closure unblocks the 24 fast-suite failures so Sprint 22 closes with a fully-green fast suite); alternatively US-269 (S; one-liner hygiene closure; ~10 min job). Both are mechanical schema-sync work, no design debate.

AUDIT FINDING: spec's `vcell=None` and `threshold=None` hypothesis modes were AUDITED and REJECTED as non-silent -- both currently TypeError on threshold comparison and are caught loud by hardware_manager._powerDownTickLoop's except-Exception handler (ERROR log, not silent bail). Per the behavior-unchanged invariant NO new defensive guards were added; spec test names re-mapped to actual silent-bail guards in test docstrings. Verification: tests/pi/power/test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py 5/5 PASS in 51.78s; pi/power + pi/hardware + pi/diagnostics + pi/integration subset 363 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 124.32s (zero regressions; this proves US-266 did not perturb US-265's tick-thread health-check tests or any orchestrator state-machine tests). Runtime-validation gate: temporarily reverted DEBUG logs in orchestrator.py, re-ran 5 tests, confirmed 4 discriminator tests FAILED with `bailMessages == []` and happy-path negative-invariant still PASSED, then restored DEBUG logs and re-confirmed 5/5 pass. Ruff clean (one auto-fix for I001 import-sort on the new test file). sprint_lint US-266 OK. Discriminator-B now fully wired alongside US-265's discriminator-A liveness probe: post-Drain-7 a `journalctl | grep 'tick early-return:'` will name which guard (if any) swallowed the BATTERY -> WARNING transition; combined with logger CSV's pd_tick_count + pd_stage columns the truth-table is now fully observable per Spool's spec. Pattern carryforward: paired-message log structure (every bail log includes BOTH the bail-causing value AND irrelevant-but-correlatable values like vcell+source) lets a post-mortem tie the orchestrator-level bail to the simultaneous drain_forensics CSV row at the (timestamp, pd_tick_count, pd_stage, vcell_v) level. Sprint 22 status now: 5/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265 + US-266); remaining 3 unclaimed -- US-267 (S, P0; discriminator C = stage-row fsync + audit error propagation -- the natural NEXT pick because US-265 + US-266 + US-267 form Spool's discriminator trio and Drain-7's truth-table verdict needs all three lit) + US-268 (M, P0; TD-042 mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures + brings fast suite to fully-green) + US-269 (S, P0; TD-044 one-liner SemVer-shape regex). ALL 15 would FAIL against pre-fix code per runtime-validation gate -- AttributeError on missing _checkTickThreadHealth + tickHealthAlarmCount, TypeError on missing tickHealthCheckIntervalS kwarg; ~430 lines), tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py UPDATE (scope-extension: 5-line state-stub addition x3 test methods that bypass __init__ via __new__ and only set bare-minimum attributes the loop reads; my tickHealthCheckIntervalS state addition broke them on the entry log; this is a direct-consequence test update of an API change, not tangential drift -- documented inline). Verification: tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py 15/15 PASS in 21.87s; pi/{hardware,power,orchestrator,diagnostics,integration} subset 452 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 149.68s (pre-touch was 437 + 3 staged-shutdown regressions; +15 new + 0 regressions); full fast suite 3833 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1006.30s (16:46) -- baseline (Session 136 per US-264) was 3818 pass / 26 fail, delta +15 = exactly the new test count + ZERO regressions; the 26 failures unchanged TD-042 + TD-044 set (closed by US-268 + US-269 later this sprint). Ruff clean across all 4 touched files. sprint_lint US-265 OK (only carryover warning is US-263 sizing). Important runtime-validation note: discriminator test test_loopOnBatteryWithNoopTick_raisesAlarm mocks orchestrator.tick at the right level per Sprint 19 lesson (Mock with no side_effect so tickCount stays 0); a no-op tick simulates exactly the hypothesis-A pathology -- thread runs the loop body but counter never advances. Pre-Drain-7 this discriminator surface is now fully wired: when CIO runs Drain Test 7 with US-262 logger active, the journalctl ERROR line `PowerDownOrchestrator tick liveness alarm: tickCount=N unchanged across X.Xs while on BATTERY` will fire 60s after the first health-check window if hypothesis A is the bug; concurrent with the logger CSV's pd_tick_count column staying at 0. If that line does NOT appear in journalctl AND pd_tick_count IS incrementing in the CSV, hypothesis A is RULED OUT and the bug is hypothesis B (US-266) or C (US-267). Pattern carryforward: paired-3-way-symmetry log strategy (lifecycle audit + spawn-site daemon+ident + loop entry tid) lets a post-incident forensic walk in journalctl prove EXACTLY which side of the boundary failed -- reusable for any future cross-module thread-startup instrumentation. Sprint 22 status now: 4/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265); remaining 4 unclaimed -- US-266 + US-267 discriminator pair (M/S; the other two hypothesis fixes; runtime-validation gate critical for Drain-7 truth-table) + US-268 TD-042 (M; mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures) + US-269 TD-044 (S; one-liner SemVer-shape regex on test_migration_0005).
